name: cuda-index-width
tagline: Guides choosing 32-bit vs 64-bit index math in PyTorch CUDA kernels, with fix patterns and a review checklist.
verdict: Strong technical advisory skill with a clear decision framework, worked code examples, and no security issues. Invokes non-universal tools (nm, rg, compute-sanitizer, GNU stat) with no requirements section or availability checks; also lacks NOT-for scope guidance and any version/changelog signal.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' or exclusion text; skill covers a range of CUDA indexing scenarios where misfire on adjacent kernel work is plausible.
No guidance on what to do if the build fails or compute-sanitizer reports errors during the A/B and test steps.
Invokes nm, rg, and compute-sanitizer ("compute-sanitizer --tool memcheck --error-exitcode=99 <python> repro.py") with no requirements section or availability check.
